cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So, Anthony went to the amusement park today, and we need to find out between what times he could have gone. The answer options are given, but I need to go through the clues step by step. First, let me list out all the information provided: 1. Anthony woke up at 6am. 2. Leslie saw Anthony reading at the library from 6am to 8am. 3. Jennifer saw Anthony working out at the gym from 8am to 9am. 4. Anthony saw Anthony buying cookies at a bakery from 2pm to 9pm. 5. William saw Anthony buying a bike at the bike shop from 9pm to 10pm. 6. The amusement park was closed after 10pm. So, the question is, when could Anthony have gone to the amusement park? Let's break this down. We need to find a time window when Anthony wasn't doing any of the listed activities, and also before the amusement park closed at 10pm. First, let's map out Anthony's known schedule: - 6am: Wakes up. - 6am to 8am: At the library reading (Leslie saw him). - 8am to 9am: At the gym working out (Jennifer saw him). - 2pm to 9pm: Buying cookies at the bakery (Anthony saw Anthony? That seems odd, but maybe it's a typo? Or perhaps it's a red herring. Maybe it's supposed to be another person, like "Michael saw Anthony..." but as given, it's Anthony saw Anthony. Hmm. Well, let's take it at face value. So Anthony was at the bakery from 2pm to 9pm buying cookies. Wait, buying cookies from 2pm to 9pm? That's a long time, but okay, maybe he's there for several hours.) - 9pm to 10pm: At the bike shop buying a bike (William saw him). Also, the park is closed after 10pm, so Anthony had to go before that. So let's plot the timeline: - 6am to 8am: Library - 8am to 9am: Gym - Then, from 9am to 2pm: What's happening? There's no mention of any activity here. After 9am, the next activity is at 2pm at the bakery. So from 9am to 2pm, Anthony is unaccounted for. Then from 2pm to 9pm: At bakery. Then 9pm to 10pm: At bike shop. So possible time windows when Anthony is free: - After waking up at 6am but before library? Well, he woke up at 6am, but Leslie saw him at the library from 6am to 8am, so he was there starting at 6am. So no time before 6am because he woke up then, and the park is presumably open after he wakes up. - From 8am to 9am? No, he's at the gym from 8 to 9am. - From 9am until 2pm: No activities reported. That's a 5-hour window. - From 9pm to 10pm: He's at the bike shop. But the park closes at 10pm, so he can't go after that. Wait, but the bakery is from 2pm to 9pm. So until 9pm he's at the bakery, and after that, he's at the bike shop until 10pm. So the only free time is between 9am and 2pm. So the amusement park visit must have happened during that window. Because during all other times, he's accounted for. So the answer would be option B: 9am to 2pm. Let me check the options again: A) 9pm to 10pm – But he's at the bike shop then. B) 9am to 2pm – Free time. C)
